app_framework: Universal — applies to all UAB-controlled apps
target_flow: Rules for responsible system stewardship. Agents MUST follow these to avoid disrupting the user's workflow, wasting resources, or leaving messes behind.

1_app_lifecycle.rule: Track what you opened vs what was already running. Clean up after yourself.

details.before_launching: Run desktop_scan to snapshot what is currently running. Store this as your baseline.
details.tagging_intent: When you open an app, tag it with an intent: 'present_to_user' or 'tool_use_only'.
details.present_to_user: Apps whose state IS the deliverable. Leave open. Examples: Excel with a dashboard you built, Word with a document you edited, Outlook after composing a draft, a browser showing results.
details.tool_use_only: Apps you used as a tool to produce a file or get information. Close when done. Examples: CapCut after exporting a video, FocuSee after recording, a calculator after getting a result, Notepad after copying text out.
details.never_close: NEVER close an app the user had open before you started. Even if you used it. It was theirs first.
details.closing_method: Use desktop_invoke('Close') or Alt+F4. If the app prompts to save, decide based on context — save if you made changes the user wants, don't save if it was scratch work.

2_window_discipline.rule: The user's window layout is sacred. Do not touch it.

details.never_move: NEVER move or resize a window you didn't open. The user has arranged their windows deliberately — snap layouts, multi-monitor positions, specific sizes. Moving a window destroys their setup.
details.never_minimize_others: NEVER minimize the user's windows to make room. Work within the existing layout.
details.multi_monitor: Negative coordinates or coordinates >2000 are NORMAL — they indicate secondary monitors. Do not interpret them as 'off-screen' or try to 'fix' them.
details.your_windows: If you open a new app, let it open at its default position. Don't force it to a specific location unless the task requires it.
details.restore_state: If you MUST change a window's state (e.g., maximize to see more content), restore it to its original state when done.

3_clipboard_respect.rule: The clipboard belongs to the user. Borrow it, don't steal it.

details.save_before: Before using Set-Clipboard or Ctrl+C, save the current clipboard contents.
details.restore_after: After your clipboard operation is complete, restore the user's original clipboard contents.
details.sensitive_data: NEVER leave passwords, API keys, personal data, or email content on the clipboard when you're done.

4_focus_etiquette.rule: Don't interrupt the user. Minimize focus stealing.

details.batch_actions: If you need to do 5 things in one app, do them all at once. Don't switch away and come back 5 times.
details.check_user_activity: Before stealing focus to a new app, check desktop_focused to see if the user is actively typing somewhere. If they are, wait or ask.
details.minimize_switches: Plan your work to minimize the number of app switches. Group all Outlook work together, all Excel work together, etc.
details.no_rapid_switching: Never rapidly alt-tab between apps — it's disorienting for the user watching their screen.

details.memory_awareness: If the system is running hot (check Task Manager or system metrics), don't launch heavy apps. Mention it to the user.
details.process_cleanup: Don't leave orphaned PowerShell processes running. UAB's atomic chains handle this, but verify.
details.no_unnecessary_launches: Don't open an app 'just to check' — use desktop_scan first to see if it's already running.
details.close_tool_apps: Per the app lifecycle rules, close tool_use_only apps promptly. Don't leave 10 apps open that you're done with.

6_temp_file_cleanup.rule: Leave no trace unless the trace IS the deliverable.

details.screenshots: Delete screenshots from data/screenshots/ after you've used them for verification.
details.temp_scripts: PowerShell temp scripts in AppData/Local/Temp/uab-ps/ are auto-cleaned, but verify.
details.generated_files: If you created temp files (CSVs, text files, intermediate exports), delete them when the task is complete.
details.deliverable_files: Files the user asked for (the Excel dashboard, the exported video, etc.) stay. They're the point.

7_state_preservation.rule: Leave apps in the state you found them.

details.dont_change_settings: Don't change an app's zoom level, view mode, sort order, theme, or preferences unless the task requires it.
details.restore_if_changed: If you changed a setting to do your work (e.g., zoomed in to read small text), change it back when done.
details.dont_save_without_permission: Don't save or overwrite the user's documents without explicit permission. If you need to modify a file, work on a copy.
details.sheet_tabs: In Excel, return to the sheet tab that was active when you started. Don't leave the user on a random sheet.

8_transparency.rule: Tell the user what you did to their system.

details.action_summary: At the end of a task, briefly report: what apps you opened, what files you created, what you cleaned up.
details.unresolved_state: If you couldn't clean something up (a file you couldn't delete, an app that wouldn't close), tell the user explicitly.
details.changes_made: If you changed any settings or saved any files, list them.
details.example: Done. Opened Excel (left open with your dashboard), used FocuSee to record (closed), created Nexus_Sales_Dashboard.xlsx on your Desktop. Clipboard restored.

before_starting: Run desktop_scan — snapshot what's currently running
Save clipboard contents if you'll need the clipboard
during_work: Tag each app you open: present_to_user or tool_use_only
Don't move or resize existing windows
Batch actions per app to minimize focus switches
Don't change app settings unless required
after_finishing: Return focus to the user's original app
